solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def latestTimeCatchTheBus(self, buses: List[int], passengers: List[int], capacity: int) -> int:
        buses.sort()
        passengers.sort()
        p = 0
        taken = set(passengers)
        for t in buses:
            cap = capacity
            while p < len(passengers) and passengers[p] <= t and cap > 0:
                p += 1
                cap -= 1
        best = 0
        if cap > 0:
            best = buses[-1]
            while best in taken:
                best -= 1
        else:
            best = passengers[p - 1] - 1
            while best in taken:
                best -= 1
        return best
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n + m log m)
space complexity: O(m)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sort buses and passengers. Simulate boarding passengers onto each bus in order. After the last bus, if it has spare capacity the latest possible arrival is the bus departure time (adjusted for passenger collisions). Otherwise, the latest arrival is just before the last passenger who boarded that bus (again adjusted for collisions).
